title: cynanchica affinis
intro: Cynanchica affinis is a plant species commonly known as the "Indian squill" or "Malabar squill," belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating various skin conditions like wounds and eczema. In terms of herbal preparations, Cynanchica affinis is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally or applied topically to reap its therapeutic benefits.
